title: Build 2026: From observability to ROI for AI agents on any framework
annotation: Trace/Evaluate/Monitor/Optimize loop; AZD observability dev experience

title: About GitHub Copilot code review
annotation: review effort levels, agentic context gathering, agentic capabilities

title: Using GitHub Copilot code review
annotation: `copilot-instructions.md`, agent skills, MCP servers

title: Copilot code review now runs on an agentic architecture
annotation: Mar 2026 changelog

title: 60 million Copilot code reviews and counting — continuous-evaluation loop tuned on accuracy/signal/speed; usage has "grown 10X" since the April 2025 launch, "now accounting for more than one in five code reviews on GitHub," surfaces actionable feedback in 71% of reviews (~5.1 comments/review), and the agentic-architecture shift alone drove an initial 8.1% increase in positive feedback
What's new with GitHub Copilot coding agent
annotation: self-review via Copilot code review before opening PR, built-in code/secret/dependency scanning, custom agents

title: Agentic Platform Engineering with GitHub Copilot
annotation: Azure DevBlogs; observe → diagnose → remediate, Copilot CLI in CI/CD

title: DevOps Playbook for the Agentic Era
annotation: pipelines as verifiers not gatekeepers; spec-driven development; agentic maturity model

title: microsoft/agentic-sdlc-starter
annotation: spec-driven AI SDLC reference architecture; assessor → specifier → generator → validator gates

title: Agentic DevOps: Evolving software development with GitHub Copilot and Microsoft Azure
annotation: agentic DevOps overview; agents across the full lifecycle

title: TestPilot
annotation: Copilot-model-driven unit-test generation with meaningful assertions; measures statement coverage

title: Copilot Workspace
annotation: plan/brainstorm/repair agents; discusses the "evaluation cost" of large AI-generated changes; repair agent fixes failing tests

title: Copilot for Pull Requests
annotation: AI test-gap detection / "GenTest", AI-powered PR repair for missing tests, docs, linter errors

title: Eval-Driven Development
annotation: manifesto/landing site; "build evals first; code is generated, evals are engineered"

title: OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions
annotation: vendor-neutral `gen_ai.*` agent telemetry standard referenced across Google, Microsoft, Databricks, and LangChain agent-observability tooling

title: Introducing SWE-bench Verified (OpenAI, Aug 13, 2024) — the standard coding-agent benchmark used by all major labs; a 500-sample human-verified subset of the original 2,294-instance SWE-bench across 12 Python repositories
Repomix: Pack your codebase into AI-friendly formats
annotation: Packs one or more repos into a single token-optimized file (XML/MD/JSON) with Tree-sitter compression and an MCP server — whole-repo, and by packing several, cross-repo context within an LLM's window.

title: CodeGraph: Pre-indexed knowledge graph for AI code context
annotation: Builds a local SQLite index of symbols, dependencies and call paths; an MCP tool returns relevant source plus call paths and a blast-radius summary in one call. Per-project multi-repo via projectPath.

title: code2prompt: Convert your codebase into a single LLM prompt
annotation: Rust CLI that turns a repo into one token-counted prompt with templating; also ships an MCP server and Python SDK, bridging the packing approach into agent/RAG workflows.

title: Serena: Semantic MCP toolkit for coding agents
annotation: Symbol-level semantic retrieval and editing over LSP backends (40+ languages), served to any client via MCP — targeted context on demand instead of whole-repo dumps, with no extra infrastructure.

title: code-review-graph: Multi-repo code knowledge graph over MCP
annotation: Tree-sitter AST to a local SQLite graph; ~30 MCP tools (impact radius, review context). A multi-repo registry plus a background daemon watch several repos at once, with cross-repo search.

title: Claude Context: Semantic code-search MCP for Claude Code
annotation: Hybrid BM25 + dense-vector search over AST-chunked code, retrieved on demand over MCP (~40% token reduction). Requires an external vector DB plus an embedding provider.

title: Aider's repository map: PageRank-ranked codebase context
annotation: An in-tool (not MCP) pre-indexed context mechanism: Tree-sitter symbol extraction plus PageRank over a file-dependency graph, token-budgeted — a reference design for surfacing just the relevant symbols.

title: Sourcegraph / Cody: Cross-repository context via SCIP code graph
annotation: Strongest cross-repo / solution-level context: true SCIP-indexed navigation across repository boundaries, scaling to 300k+ repos. Full multi-repo is largely Enterprise-tier.

title: Git Context Controller: Git-like memory management for LLM agents
annotation: Research framework for the git-as-memory / context-repository pattern: manage agent context with COMMIT/BRANCH/MERGE/CONTEXT operations over a versioned .GCC/ file tree.

title: OpenSpec: Spec-driven development for AI coding assistants
annotation: Lightweight, MIT-licensed SDD framework for AI coding assistants (Fission-AI); /opsx slash-command workflow, npm @fission-ai/openspec.

title: Qodo-Cover: AI agent for automated test generation and coverage
annotation: AI test-generation agent wiring a test-runner + coverage-parser feedback loop (AGPL-3.0; formerly CodiumAI Cover-Agent); repo deprecated / 'no longer maintained' 2025-06-15.

title: NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails: Programmable guardrails for LLM systems
annotation: NVIDIA open-source guardrail/policy engine; five rail categories (input/dialog/retrieval/execution/output), execution rails cover tool calls (Apache-2.0).

title: Guardrails AI: Input/output validation framework for LLM applications
annotation: Python framework for LLM input/output validation + structured generation; Input/Output Guards, 65+ Hub validators, Pydantic/RAIL schema (Apache-2.0).

title: Large Language Models for Unit Test Generation
annotation: arXiv survey documenting the generate-validate-repair paradigm — execution feedback fed back to the LLM for self-correction.

title: E2B: Secure cloud sandboxes for AI-generated code
annotation: Open-source infrastructure to run AI-generated code in isolated Firecracker-microVM cloud sandboxes (~200ms start) — kernel-level isolation for untrusted, agent-produced code.

title: Firecracker: Lightweight microVM monitor for isolated execution
annotation: AWS-built VMM that runs workloads in lightweight KVM microVMs — hardware-virtualization isolation with container-like speed; the isolation primitive under E2B, AWS Lambda and Fargate.

title: gVisor: Application-kernel container sandbox (runsc)
annotation: Google's userspace application kernel (memory-safe Go) shipping the OCI runtime runsc; integrates with Docker/Kubernetes to sandbox containers, since ordinary containers share the host kernel and can be escaped by a single vulnerability.

title: Auth0 for AI Agents: Token Vault & human-in-the-loop approval
annotation: Identity layer for agents: a Token Vault brokers scoped per-user OAuth tokens for external APIs through one integration, with CIBA asynchronous human approval before sensitive actions and granular least-privilege permissions.

title: Arcade.dev: OAuth tool authorization for AI agents
annotation: Runtime/authorization layer where each agent tool declares its required OAuth 2.0 scopes, enforcing user-delegated, least-privilege, just-in-time access on a user's behalf instead of broad service accounts.

title: Infisical Agent Vault: Credential proxy for AI agents
annotation: HTTP credential proxy that intercepts an agent's outbound requests and injects real secrets before forwarding, so the agent uses placeholder values and never holds or sees the actual credentials.

title: HashiCorp Vault: Agentic runtime security & per-agent identity
annotation: Gives every AI agent a verifiable identity at deployment, tracked across on-prem/cloud/hybrid, with dynamic short-lived secrets — non-human identity management for agentic runtimes.

title: MCP-Scan / Snyk Agent Scan: Security scanner for MCP servers & agents
annotation: Connects to MCP servers, retrieves tool descriptions, and scans agents/MCP servers/skills for prompt injection, tool poisoning, tool shadowing, toxic flows and hardcoded secrets. Acquired by Snyk (June 2025) and rebranded 'Agent Scan'.

title: Cisco AI Defense MCP Scanner
annotation: Python tool that scans MCP servers/tools for security findings using a multi-engine approach — Cisco AI Defense inspect API, YARA rules and an LLM-as-a-judge, runnable together or independently — detecting prompt injection, tool poisoning and ~12 other vectors.

title: LlamaFirewall: Open-source guardrail framework for AI agents
annotation: Layered guardrail framework (PromptGuard 2, Agent Alignment Checks, CodeShield) that detects and mitigates AI-centric risks across LLM chat and multi-step agentic operations. NB: its indirect/obfuscated-injection-in-tool-responses coverage was not confirmed in verification.

title: OWASP Agentic AI: Threats & Mitigations (T1–T15)
annotation: The canonical agentic threat vocabulary: 15 numbered threats each paired with mitigations — T1 Memory Poisoning, T2 Tool Misuse, T3 Privilege Compromise, T8 Repudiation & Untraceability, T10 Overwhelming Human-in-the-Loop, T11 Unexpected RCE & Code Attacks, T13 Rogue Agents.

title: OWASP Top 10 for Agentic Applications (2026)
annotation: Prioritized risk list for autonomous agents (released 10 Dec 2025): ASI01 Agent Behavior/Goal Hijacking, ASI02 Tool Misuse & Exploitation, ASI03 Identity & Privilege Abuse — mapping directly to tool-scoping/allowlist and least-privilege controls.

title: Claude Code: Permissions & permission modes
annotation: Primary source for several safe-autonomy patterns: discrete permission modes (plan → acceptEdits → bypassPermissions), a three-tier allow/ask/deny rule system (deny → ask → allow, first match wins), and harness-enforced (out-of-model) permission checks that prompt-injected instructions cannot override.

title: Claude Code auto mode: model-graded action approvals
annotation: Delegates per-action approvals to a model-based transcript classifier — a middle ground between manual review and --dangerously-skip-permissions — plus tool-output sanitization and dropping arbitrary-code-execution rules on entering auto mode. Disclosed research preview (~17% of overeager actions slip through).

title: OpenAI Codex: Agent approvals & sandboxing
annotation: Codex's approval modes and OS-level sandboxing: read-only / auto / full-access tiers gate state-changing actions, while sandboxing restricts filesystem and network reach as a complementary defense layer.

title: AAGATE: A NIST AI RMF-aligned governance platform for agentic AI
annotation: Research-stage platform that operationalizes the NIST AI RMF with a named framework per function (CSA MAESTRO for Map; AIVSS+SSVC for Measure; CSA Agentic Red Teaming for Manage), guardrails-as-code (NL→Rego/OPA), and the Janus shadow-monitor plan-then-execute pattern. Design, not measured efficacy.

title: Design patterns for securing LLM agents against prompt injection (lethal trifecta, dual-LLM)
annotation: Simon Willison's catalog of six prompt-injection design patterns (action-selector, plan-then-execute, dual-LLM, map-reduce, code-then-execute, context-minimization) plus the 'lethal trifecta' framing (private data + untrusted content + exfiltration). Surfaced during research but only as editorial framing in the verified corpus — include with that caveat.

title: CaMeL: Defeating prompt injections by design
annotation: Capabilities-based design that extracts a control/data-flow plan from the trusted user query and enforces a security policy on tool calls, structurally separating trusted instructions from untrusted data. Surfaced during research but not independently verified as a finding this pass.

title: OpenAI Agents SDK - Agent orchestration
annotation: OpenAI's official agent framework (MIT; successor to Swarm). Splits orchestration into LLM-driven vs code-driven and offers two multi-agent patterns: handoffs and agents-as-tools.

title: langgraph-supervisor: Hierarchical multi-agent systems on LangGraph
annotation: LangChain library (MIT) implementing the supervisor/hierarchical pattern over LangGraph; an output_mode parameter controls how much worker context flows back.

title: Temporal: Durable execution for AI agents
annotation: Durable-execution engine: records an Event History and replays it to resume after a crash, holding workflow state for years without hand-built state machines.

title: Restate: Durable execution for agentic loops
annotation: Framework-agnostic durable execution: journals intermediate step results and replays to recover to the point of failure; demonstrated across OpenAI Agents SDK, Vercel AI SDK, ADK, LangChain.

title: Dapr Agents: Durable agentic workflows
annotation: CNCF project (Apache-2.0): a DurableAgent over Dapr Workflows supporting the full pattern set, with three pluggable memory backends (in-memory / vector / Dapr state store).

title: Inngest: Durable execution for AI agents
annotation: Durable-execution platform positioning step-level checkpointing and automatic retries as the substrate for reliable agent loops. (Vendor blog — backfill; not separately verified.)

title: OpenAI Swarm: Lightweight multi-agent orchestration (educational)
annotation: OpenAI's experimental, educational multi-agent framework built on routines + handoffs; now superseded by the production Agents SDK. (Backfill for lineage.)

title: Managed Agents
annotation: Infrastructure-level orchestration pattern that decouples the agent's 'brain' (model + harness) from its 'hands' (sandboxes/tools) and 'session' (event log) so each can scale, fail, and recover independently.

title: OpenAI Agents SDK: Handoffs
annotation: Handoffs as a first-class, model-directed routing mechanism (transfer_to_<agent>); by default the receiving agent sees the entire prior history unless filtered — a named context-pollution lever.

title: Don't Build Multi-Agents
annotation: Cognition's (Devin) contrarian case for single-threaded, continuous-context agents over multi-agent topologies for coding: 'share context' and beware conflicting implicit decisions. (Vendor blog/opinion — backfill.)

title: Why Do Multi-Agent LLM Systems Fail?
annotation: Empirical MAST failure taxonomy (Cemri et al., UC Berkeley): 14 failure modes across specification, inter-agent misalignment, and task verification. (arXiv preprint — backfill; treat as early research.)

title: DeepEval: Pytest-style LLM eval framework
annotation: Open-source (Apache-2.0) Pytest-style LLM eval framework; 50+ metrics (G-Eval/DAG LLM-judge, RAG faithfulness/contextual precision-recall, agentic Task Completion/Tool Correctness/Plan Adherence); `deepeval test run` CI gates; Confident AI MCP server lets Claude Code/Cursor run evals.

title: Promptfoo: CI/CD eval runner & quality gate
annotation: MIT CLI + official GitHub Action; runs prompt/agent/RAG evals on PR/push; gates via --fail-on-error and fail-on-threshold (0-100%); supports GitLab CI, Jenkins, Azure Pipelines, CircleCI, Bitbucket, Travis.

title: Braintrust eval-action: evals in CI with PR-comment regressions
annotation: Official GitHub Action runs `braintrust eval` and posts a Score/Average/Improvements/Regressions PR comment linking the dashboard. Open-source action; Braintrust platform commercial.

title: Guardrails AI
annotation: Apache-2.0 Python framework; Input/Output Guards that detect, quantify and mitigate LLM risks, plus a Guardrails Hub of validators. Runtime guardrail/validation layer.

title: NVIDIA NeMo Guardrails
annotation: Apache-2.0 NVIDIA toolkit for programmable guardrails (Colang input/output/dialog/retrieval/execution rails). Surfaced in the quality-gates search but not independently verified as a finding this pass.

title: Datadog LLM Observability: native OpenTelemetry GenAI support
annotation: Commercial; natively supports OpenTelemetry GenAI Semantic Conventions (v1.37+), auto-mapping gen_ai.* attributes; OTLP-based ingest via direct exporter, Datadog Agent, or OTel Collector.

title: OpenLLMetry (Traceloop): OTel-based LLM instrumentation
annotation: Apache-2.0 OpenTelemetry instrumentations emitting gen_ai.* spans (prompts, completions, token usage) to any OTel backend. (Claim that Traceloop leads the OTel LLM working group was refuted.)

title: LangSmith: framework-agnostic agent tracing & observability
annotation: Commercial; step-by-step agent trace/span capture + token/cost/latency (P50/P99); SDKs (Python/TS/Go/Java) plus OpenTelemetry; works beyond LangChain (OpenAI/Anthropic/Vercel AI/LlamaIndex).

title: Inside the LLM Call: GenAI Observability with OpenTelemetry
annotation: OSS (OpenTelemetry/CNCF). An agent run surfaces as a top-level invoke_agent span with child chat and execute_tool spans; standardized gen_ai.* token/latency attributes. VS Code Copilot, OpenAI Codex, and Claude Code emit OTel GenAI telemetry. Semconv pages still experimental.

title: Introducing Agent Evaluation in Vertex AI Gen AI evaluation service
annotation: Vertex AI groups agent metrics into final-response vs trajectory; ships six trajectory metrics (exact/in-order/any-order match, precision, recall, single-tool-use). Trajectory precision/recall are tool-trajectory metrics, not RAG retrieval.

title: Evaluating AI Agents: a practical guide with Microsoft Foundry
annotation: Two-loop eval model (inner pre-ship CI gates / outer post-ship production); prioritize outcomes over paths; a failing eval blocks the merge, with statistical analysis to separate real regressions from run-to-run noise.

title: Continuous evaluation of agents (Microsoft Foundry)
annotation: Continuously evaluates live agent interactions at a configurable sampling rate (AgentEvaluationSamplingConfiguration); metrics on the Foundry Observability dashboard; evaluations linked to traces for root-cause. Some features public preview.

title: Context Rot: how increasing input tokens impacts LLM performance
annotation: Chroma (2025) controlled study across 18 models: performance grows unreliable as input length grows, often before the window limit, and worse as query-target similarity drops; focused prompts beat full ones, evidence that curating context improves accuracy.

title: Justice or Prejudice? Quantifying Biases in LLM-as-a-Judge (CALM)
annotation: Peer-reviewed (ICLR 2025): identifies twelve LLM-judge bias types; all judges significantly affected by position bias; self-enhancement model-dependent. Basis for judge-calibration and order-swap mitigations.

title: A Survey on LLM-as-a-Judge
annotation: Formalizes LLM-as-a-judge as E <- P_LLM(x (+) C); catalogues position/verbosity/self-enhancement biases and mitigations (e.g., swap contents and average in pairwise evaluation).

title: Judging the Judges: evaluating alignment and vulnerabilities in LLMs-as-judges
annotation: Peer-reviewed: position bias in LLM judges is systematic, not random (MT-Bench lineage); judges need human calibration before gating releases or feeding training.

title: LangSmith: trajectory evaluations
annotation: Vendor docs: score the full sequence of agent steps/tool calls (trajectory) vs only the final answer, tooling for trajectory-vs-final-answer evaluation.

title: Arize Phoenix: open AI observability & evaluation
annotation: Open-source AI observability/eval platform under Elastic License 2.0 (source-available, not OSI-approved). OpenTelemetry via OpenInference; Python/TS SDKs; MCP server (@arizeai/phoenix-mcp) plus Claude Code/Cursor skills. General LLM apps + coding agents.

title: Helicone: open AI gateway & LLM observability
annotation: AI gateway + LLM observability (trace/session inspection, cost/latency/quality analytics, prompt versioning) routing across 100+ models. Core repo Apache-2.0; separate ai-gateway component GPL-3.0. General LLM apps.

title: Weights & Biases Weave: tracing & evaluation toolkit
annotation: Apache-2.0 toolkit to log/debug LLM inputs/outputs/traces and build evaluations; accepts OpenTelemetry trace data via a dedicated OTLP HTTP endpoint. General LLM apps.

title: Inspect: UK AISI framework for LLM & agent evals
annotation: MIT framework by the UK AI Security Institute (with Meridian Labs) for LLM/agent evals; built for coding & agentic tasks; supports MCP tools and runs Claude Code/Codex CLI/Gemini CLI in Docker/Kubernetes sandboxes.

title: Inspect Evals: community benchmark suite on Inspect
annotation: MIT repository of dozens of community benchmark evals built on Inspect AI (UK AISI, Arcadia Impact, Vector Institute); includes coding-agent benchmarks (SWE-Bench, Cybench) plus GAIA, GDM CTF. Benchmarking, not production observability.
